doc_parsing_guide.overview:
This rule defines procedures for reading IBM Knowledge Center / IBM Documentation pages (ibm.com/docs) efficiently — minimising token cost, avoiding JavaScript rendering overhead, and correctly resolving child page links.
Two categories of page exist on ibm.com/docs: 1. Static API pages — served as raw DITA HTML by the content API endpoint (no JS needed) 2. JS-rendered pages — served by the Next.js frontend (require a browser to render)
The IBM Documentation content API makes most pages available as static HTML without a browser. This is the preferred approach: lower latency, lower token cost, no headless browser required.

ibm_docs_content_api.description: IBM Documentation exposes a content API that serves raw DITA HTML for each topic. This avoids the JavaScript rendering layer entirely and returns clean, parseable HTML.
ibm_docs_content_api.endpoint_pattern:
https://www.ibm.com/docs/api/v1/content/{lang}/{product}/{version}?topic={slug}
Where: lang = en (English) or other locale product = product identifier, e.g. app-connect version = version string, e.g. 13.0.x slug = topic slug as it appears in the browser URL ?topic= parameter
Example (IBM ACE 13.0.x — ibmint generate tests command): https://www.ibm.com/docs/api/v1/content/en/app-connect/13.0.x?topic=commands-ibmint-generate-tests-command
The slug MUST be taken from the browser URL — it cannot be derived from the DITA HTML filename. See Section 3 for how to resolve child page slugs.
ibm_docs_content_api.response_format: The API returns raw DITA HTML — a complete <html> document with: - Minimal <head> (meta tags, charset, title, link rels for navigation) - <body> containing the topic content as semantic HTML - <a href="filename_.html"> links to related topics (bare filenames, NOT slugs) - No JavaScript, no CSS framework, no dynamic content Response size is typically 3 000–20 000 chars per page (vs 50 000–100 000 for JS-rendered).
ibm_docs_content_api.success_detection:
A valid content API response: - Has HTTP 200 status - Contains <body id="..."> (the DITA topic ID) - Does NOT contain "primaryCategory": "error" (IBM 404 page marker) - Is typically 3 000+ characters
A 404 response: - Still returns HTTP 200 but contains "primaryCategory": "error" - Contains "IBM notice: The page you requested cannot be displayed" - Check: $html -notmatch '"primaryCategory":\s*"error"'

powershell_fetch_procedure.description: Standard procedure to fetch and clean an IBM docs page on Windows
powershell_fetch_procedure.code: # Step 1: Fetch the raw DITA HTML via the content API $slug = "commands-ibmint-generate-tests-command" # from browser URL ?topic= parameter $url = "https://www.ibm.com/docs/api/v1/content/en/app-connect/13.0.x?topic=$slug" $html = (Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $url -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 30).Content # Step 2: Check it is not a 404 if ($html -match '"primaryCategory":\s*"error"') { Write-Error "Topic not found: $slug" return } # Step 3: Strip HTML tags to extract clean text $text = $html -replace '(?s)]*>.*?', '' $text = $text -replace '(?s)]*>.*?', '' $text = $text -replace '', "`n" $text = $text -replace ']*>', "`n- " $text = $text -replace ']*>', "`n### " $text = $text -replace '', '' $text = $text -replace ']*>', "`n" $text = $text -replace ']*>', '`' $text = $text -replace '', '`' $text = $text -replace ']*>', "`n``````" $text = $text -replace '', "``````" $text = $text -replace '<[^>]+>', '' $text = $text -replace '&', '&' $text = $text -replace '<', '<' $text = $text -replace '>', '>' $text = $text -replace ' ', ' ' $text = $text -replace ''', "'" $text = $text -replace ' +', ' ' $text = [regex]::Replace($text, '(\r?\n){3,}', "`n`n") $text = $text.Trim() Write-Host $text

link_extraction_procedure.description: Extract all <a href> links from the raw DITA HTML. Links are bare filenames (e.g. bi13020_.html) — NOT the browser slugs needed for the API. See Section 3 to resolve filenames to slugs.
link_extraction_procedure.example_output: [Configure an integration server to record messages] -> bi13015_.html [Generate a test project by using ibmint generate tests] -> bi13020_.html [Compile the test project and create a BAR file] -> bi13025_.html [Run the tests using command line] -> bi13032_.html [Run the tests using the Toolkit] -> bi13035_.html

resolving_child_page_slugs.problem: The DITA HTML returned by the content API contains links as bare filenames: Generating tests by using the ibmint generate tests command These bare filenames do NOT work as topic slugs in the API URL. The browser slug (e.g. "dit-generating-tests-ibmint-command") is computed by IBM's JS routing layer and is NOT published via any public API. Attempting to use bare filenames as API slugs returns 404 error pages.

method.name: 1_known_slug
method.description: If you already have the browser URL for the page (from the user or from a previous successful fetch), extract the slug directly from the ?topic= parameter.

method.name: 2_slug_from_page_text
method.description: The page text itself often contains references to child pages by their full topic slug embedded in linked text. When a page lists child topics with descriptive titles, those titles can be used to guess the slug using IBM's slug conventions.
method.slug_conventions:
IBM docs slugs follow a pattern: {prefix}-{kebab-case-title} where prefix is a short category identifier (e.g. "dit", "commands", "cfg", "ts").
Examples: "Generating tests from recorded messages" → dit-generating-tests-from-recorded-messages-in-message-flow "ibmint generate tests command" → commands-ibmint-generate-tests-command "Configuring an integration server" → cfg-configuring-integration-server (guessed)
Guessing is unreliable — verify with a fetch attempt before using.

method.name: 3_probe_and_verify
method.description: Build candidate slug guesses from the link text, then probe each one with the content API. A response that does NOT contain "primaryCategory": "error" is valid.

method.name: 4_js_rendered_page_scrape
method.description:
The JS-rendered IBM docs page (fetched with Invoke-WebRequest) embeds child topic slugs in its HTML. However: the slugs found in the rendered page are from IBM's GLOBAL navigation sidebar (shared across all IBM products) — NOT the child topics of the current page. This method only works if the child slugs happen to appear in the navigation markup, which is unreliable.
Use this method as a last resort when probe-and-verify fails for all guesses.
method.code: $r = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/app-connect/13.0.x?topic=dit-generating-tests-from-recorded-messages-in-message-flow" -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 20 $slugs = [regex]::Matches($r.Content, 'topic=([a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_\-]{5,80})') | ForEach-Object { $_.Groups[1].Value } | Sort-Object -Unique # Filter to ACE-relevant slugs (exclude IBM-wide nav slugs) $slugs | Where-Object { $_ -match '^(dit|commands|cfg|ts|ac|an|bi|fm|re)' }
method.limitation: The JS-rendered page does NOT embed child topic slugs. The topic= values found are from the global IBM product navigation sidebar, not the page's child links. This approach was verified NOT to work for IBM ACE docs child links.

known_slug_patterns.description: IBM ACE 13.0.x docs use these prefix patterns for topic slugs. Use as a guide when constructing candidate slugs for probe-and-verify.

patterns.pattern: Integration testing topics (dit = Developing Integration Tests)

decision_procedure.description: Use this decision tree whenever asked to read an IBM docs URL or follow its links.

step.title: Extract the topic slug from the URL the user provided
step.action: Parse the ?topic= parameter from the browser URL. Example: https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/app-connect/13.0.x?topic=SLUG If the URL has no ?topic= parameter, it is a product homepage — fetch as-is.

step.title: Fetch the page via the content API (not the browser URL)
Clean and read the page content
step.action: Apply the HTML stripping procedure from Section 1 to extract clean text. Read the cleaned text to answer the user's question. Total token cost is typically 1 000–4 000 tokens per page.

step.title: Extract child links if required
step.action: If the user asked to "follow all links" or "read child pages", apply the link extraction procedure from Section 1 to get the list of linked filenames. Then for each child link, follow the slug discovery procedure in Section 2 (method 1 if slug is known, method 3 probe-and-verify otherwise). Fetch each valid child page using the content API.
step.token_cost_warning: Fetching all child pages multiplies token cost by the number of children. Before fetching all children, summarise what the parent page says the children cover and ask the user which ones are relevant.

worked_example.user_request: Read https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/app-connect/13.0.x?topic=dit-generating-tests-from-recorded-messages-in-message-flow and all its linked child pages.

step.action: Fetch parent page via content API
step.result: HTTP 200, 7411 chars. Not a 404. Content is raw DITA HTML.

step.action: Extract child links from DITA HTML
step.result: [Configure an integration server to record messages] -> bi13015_.html [Generating tests by using ibmint generate tests command] -> bi13020_.html [Compiling a test project and creating a BAR file] -> bi13025_.html [Importing a test project into the Toolkit] -> bi13030_.html [Run the tests using command line] -> bi13032_.html [Run the tests using the Toolkit] -> bi13035_.html

step.finding: Bare filenames (bi13015_, bi13020_, etc.) return 404 when used directly as slugs. The "commands-ibmint-generate-tests-command" slug was already known and works. Other child slugs require probing with candidate guesses based on prefix patterns.
step.known_working_slug: commands-ibmint-generate-tests-command → "Generating tests by using ibmint generate tests command"

step.action: Ask user which child pages to fetch (token cost management)
step.recommendation: Rather than fetching all 6 child pages automatically, present the list to the user and ask which are relevant. Each child page costs 1 000–4 000 tokens.

method.name: JS-rendered page (Invoke-WebRequest on browser URL)
method.after_stripping: 2 000–8 000 chars (but stripping is lossy for JS-rendered content)
method.token_cost: HIGH — large JS bundles inflate size even after tag stripping
method.reliability: LOW — content is injected by JavaScript; static fetch returns empty body
method.use_when: Last resort only — when content API returns 404 for a known-valid page

method.name: Content API (docs/api/v1/content endpoint)
method.token_cost: LOW — clean DITA HTML, no JS, no CSS frameworks
method.reliability: HIGH — content is always present in the HTML body
method.use_when: PRIMARY method for all IBM docs pages

rules.rule: ALWAYS use the content API endpoint (docs/api/v1/content) as the first approach. Never fetch the browser URL directly unless the content API returns a 404.
Save fetched HTML to a temp file (.bob/tmp_*.html) before processing. Delete temp files after extracting the needed text.
When a page has multiple child links, summarise child page titles to the user and ask which are needed BEFORE fetching them all.
For large pages (20 000+ chars), use targeted text extraction with Select-String on the cleaned text rather than passing the full text to the model context.
